candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Meyer-Schuster and Rupe rearrangements describing acid-catalyzed conversion of secondary or tertiary propargylic alcohols into alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: protic or Lewis-acid catalysis, Meyer-Schuster rearrangement of secondary/tertiary propargylic alcohols to enones or enals, Rupe rearrangement of tertiary alcohols to alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones, and mechanistic involvement of propargylic/allenyl cation equilibration.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Michael addition describing 1,4-conjugate addition of carbon or heteroatom nucleophiles to activated alkenes or alkynes bearing electron-withdrawing groups.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: conjugate addition to alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl and related acceptors, use of stabilized carbon nucleophiles or heteroatom donors, thermodynamic reversibility in protic media, and broad utility of Michael adducts in cascade synthesis.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Midland Alpine-Borane reduction describing asymmetric reduction of ketones and aldehydes with chiral B-3-alpha-pinanyl-9-BBN reagents to give enantioenriched alcohols.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: preparation of Alpine-Borane from alpha-pinene and 9-BBN, chiral hydride delivery to prochiral carbonyl compounds, substrate- and steric-effects on rate and selectivity, and mechanistic transition-state models for enantioselection.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Minisci reaction describing radical alkylation or acylation of protonated electron-deficient heteroarenes under oxidative conditions.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: generation of carbon-centered radicals from diverse precursors, addition to protonated azaarenes, reoxidation to restore aromaticity, and regioselective functionalization of pyridines, quinolines, isoquinolines, and related heterocycles.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Mislow-Evans rearrangement describing allylic sulfoxide/sulfinate-sulfoxide rearrangement chemistry that delivers allylic alcohols through reversible [2,3]-sigmatropic sulfur migration.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: thermal racemization of allylic sulfoxides, conversion of allylic alcohols to allylic sulfoxides and alcohols, stereospecific [2,3]-rearrangement through sulfinate esters, and thiophile-assisted reductive cleavage to allylic alcohol products.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Mitsunobu reaction describing inversion-based substitution of alcohols using phosphine/azodicarboxylate reagents and diverse nucleophiles.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: activation of secondary alcohols by phosphine and dialkyl azodicarboxylates, inversion of configuration, broad oxygen/nitrogen/sulfur/carbon nucleophile scope, and mechanistic participation of zwitterionic phosphonium intermediates.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for Miyaura boration describing palladium-catalyzed conversion of aryl or vinyl halides and triflates into boronic esters using bis(pinacolato)diboron or tetraalkoxydiborons.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: Pd-catalyzed borylation of aryl/vinyl electrophiles, use of B2pin2 or related diborons, importance of acetate base, compatibility with cross-coupling workflows, and catalytic-cycle steps of oxidative addition, transmetalation, and reductive elimination.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Mukaiyama aldol reaction describing Lewis-acid-promoted addition of silyl enol ethers to aldehydes or ketones to form beta-hydroxy carbonyl compounds.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: preformed enol-silane nucleophiles, Lewis acid activation of carbonyl electrophiles, anti/syn stereochemical models, catalytic asymmetric variants, and broad use in fragment coupling for polyketide synthesis.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Myers asymmetric alkylation describing highly diastereoselective alpha-alkylation of pseudoephedrine-derived amide enolates to access optically active carboxylic-acid derivatives and related products.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: pseudoephedrine auxiliary-controlled alkylation, LiCl-assisted enolate generation, broad alkyl halide scope, high alpha-face selectivity, and downstream conversion of alkylated amides into acids, aldehydes, or alcohols.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Nagata hydrocyanation describing conjugate addition of cyanide to activated alkenes or enones using dialkylaluminum cyanides or HCN/AlX3 combinations.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: 1,4-addition of cyanide to alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones and related acceptors, use of Et2AlCN or HCN/AlEt3, strong solvent and Lewis-acid effects, and hydrolysis to beta-cyano carbonyl compounds.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Nazarov cyclization describing acid- or Lewis-acid-promoted electrocyclization of divinyl ketones through pentadienyl cation intermediates to cyclopentenones.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: divinyl ketone precursors, 4π conrotatory electrocyclization, pentadienyl cation intermediates, control by beta-substituents and silicon directing groups, and stereospecific ring closure to cyclopentenones.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Neber rearrangement describing base-induced conversion of O-acylated ketoximes into alpha-amino ketones through azirine intermediates.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: preparation of O-acyl ketoximes from oximes, rearrangement under alkoxide base, competing concerted and nitrene pathways, formation of 2H-azirines, and utility for alpha-amino ketone synthesis.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Nef reaction describing conversion of nitronate salts or nitroalkanes into carbonyl compounds under acidic or oxidative conditions.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: acid-dependent hydrolysis of nitronate salts, conversion of primary and secondary nitro compounds into aldehydes or ketones, access to oximes and carbonyl products, and reductive or oxidative variants of the Nef manifold.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Negishi cross-coupling describing Pd- or Ni-catalyzed coupling of organozinc reagents with aryl, vinyl, allyl, or alkynyl electrophiles.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: organozinc nucleophiles generated by direct insertion or transmetalation, broad functional-group tolerance relative to Grignard reagents, stereoretentive cross-coupling, and catalytic cycles involving oxidative addition, transmetalation, and reductive elimination.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Nenitzescu indole synthesis describing condensation of p-benzoquinones with β-aminocrotonates or related enamine-type partners to furnish 5-hydroxyindoles.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: p-benzoquinone electrophiles, β-aminocrotonate or β-amino-α,β-unsaturated carbonyl partners, formation of substituted 5-hydroxyindoles, regioselectivity effects of quinone substitution, and a mechanistic pathway involving Michael addition followed by cyclization.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Nicholas reaction describing Lewis- or Brønsted-acid-promoted decomplexation and substitution chemistry of dicobalt hexacarbonyl-stabilized propargylic alcohol complexes.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: formation of Co2(CO)6-alkyne complexes, generation of cobalt-stabilized propargylic cations, nucleophilic trapping, oxidative/reductive decomplexation, and access to substituted alkynes or enynes.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for Noyori asymmetric hydrogenation describing BINAP-Ru-catalyzed enantioselective hydrogenation of functionalized olefins and carbonyl compounds to chiral acids, alcohols, and amines.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: BINAP-Ru catalysts, H2 pressure, high enantioselectivity for beta-keto esters and enones, dynamic kinetic resolution, and predictable stereochemical outcome with functionalized substrates.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Nozaki-Hiyama-Kishi reaction describing CrCl2-mediated coupling of allyl, vinyl, aryl, or alkynyl halides with aldehydes or ketones to form allylic or homoallylic alcohols.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: organochromium formation from organic halides under CrCl2, Ni-catalyzed variants, chemoselective carbonyl addition, stereocontrol in macrocyclizations, and broad utility in fragment coupling.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Oppenauer oxidation describing aluminum-alkoxide-mediated oxidation of secondary alcohols with ketone hydride acceptors such as acetone to give ketones or aldehydes under mild transfer-hydrogen conditions.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: reverse Meerwein-Ponndorf-Verley redox manifold, aluminum alkoxide exchange, six-membered hydride-transfer transition state, high chemoselectivity for secondary alcohol oxidation, and suppression of competing aldol/Tishchenko pathways by solvent and substrate choice.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Overman rearrangement describing thermal or mercury/palladium-catalyzed [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allylic trichloroacetimidates to allylic trichloroacetamides that can be hydrolyzed to allylic amines.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: preparation of allylic trichloroacetimidates from allylic alcohols and trichloroacetonitrile, suprafacial Claisen-type [3,3]-shift, stereospecific allylic amination, and catalytic variants for sensitive substrates.

candidates.family_name: Oxy-Cope Rearrangement and Anionic Oxy-Cope Rearrangement
candidates.page_title: OXY-COPE REARRANGEMENT AND ANIONIC OXY-COPE REARRANGEMENT
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the oxy-Cope and anionic oxy-Cope rearrangements describing [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of 1,5-dien-3-ols or their alkoxides to enols or enolates that furnish δ,ε-unsaturated carbonyl compounds after tautomerization or protonation.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: thermal vs base-accelerated variants, large rate acceleration upon alkoxide formation, chairlike transition states, stereochemical transfer through ordered cyclic TS structures, and preference for anionic variants in synthesis.
candidates.exact_pattern_family: Oxy-Cope Rearrangement and Anionic Oxy-Cope Rearrangement

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Paal-Knorr furan synthesis describing acid- or dehydrating-agent-promoted cyclodehydration of 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds or surrogates to substituted furans.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: classical mineral-acid dehydration, use of Lewis acids or phosphorus-based dehydrating agents, surrogate 1,4-dicarbonyl precursors, and mechanism involving protonation, hemiacetal formation, and water loss rather than simple monoketal dehydration.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Paal-Knorr pyrrole synthesis describing condensation of 1,4-diketones with ammonia, ammonium salts, or primary amines under protic or Lewis acidic conditions to form substituted pyrroles.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: broad amine scope including ammonia equivalents and primary amines, tolerance of diverse 1,4-dicarbonyl substitution patterns, solvent effects, and mechanistic evidence for rate-determining cyclization of a hemiaminal intermediate.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Passerini multicomponent reaction describing isocyanide-based three-component coupling of a carboxylic acid, a carbonyl compound, and an isocyanide to furnish α-acyloxycarboxamides or related heterocyclic products.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: one-pot multicomponent assembly, broad aldehyde/ketone and acid scope, isocyanide insertion into activated carbonyl–carboxylate adducts, and variants using water or hydrazoic acid to access α-hydroxycarboxamides or tetrazoles.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Paternò-Büchi reaction describing photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition of excited carbonyl compounds with alkenes to furnish oxetanes, often with predictable regioselectivity based on 1,4-biradical intermediates.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: UV-light excitation of aldehydes or ketones, singlet/triplet carbonyl pathways, alkene addition to form 1,4-biradicals, collapse to oxetanes, and stereochemical outcomes governed by substitution pattern and biradical conformation.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Pauson-Khand reaction describing cobalt-mediated formal [2+2+1] cycloaddition of an alkyne, an alkene, and carbon monoxide to generate substituted cyclopentenones.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: dicobalt-alkyne complexes, carbon monoxide insertion, alkene coordination, regioselective cyclization to cyclopentenones, and promoter-assisted variants using N-oxides, sulfides, light, or alternative metal carbonyl catalysts.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Payne rearrangement describing base-catalyzed intramolecular epoxide migration of 2,3-epoxy alcohols to isomeric epoxides, plus aza- and thia-Payne variants that access aziridino and thiirano alcohols.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: deprotonation to an alkoxide, 3-exo-tet ring opening and reclosure with inversion at C2, solvent/base dependence, epoxide equilibration, and hetero-Payne variants promoted by base or Lewis acid.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Perkin reaction describing condensation of aromatic aldehydes with acid anhydrides or N-acyl glycine derivatives under basic conditions to produce α,β-unsaturated carboxylic acids or azlactones.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: aromatic aldehyde preference, enolization of anhydrides or azlactones, β-alkoxide formation followed by elimination, mixed-anhydride variants, and utility in cinnamic-acid and α-amino-acid precursor synthesis.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Petasis boronic acid-Mannich reaction describing three-component coupling of an amine, a carbonyl compound, and a boronic acid to furnish allylic or benzylic amines under mild, often neutral conditions.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: iminium formation, boronate ate-complex generation, stereoretentive vinyl transfer from alkenylboronic acids, and expansion from formaldehyde to other aldehydes, ketones, and N-acyliminium variants.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Petasis-Ferrier rearrangement describing Lewis-acid-promoted conversion of five- and six-membered enol acetals derived from dioxolan-4-ones or dioxane-4-ones into substituted tetrahydrofurans and tetrahydropyrans.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: formation of exocyclic enol acetals with titanocene dimethylidene reagents, aluminum-mediated oxocarbenium rearrangement, carbonyl transposition, and stereocontrolled ring construction in linked-fragment assembly.
